User Story: As an agent, I want to start an async AI video generation job so that I can create video content without blocking.
Expected Behavior: Returns a job_id immediately after validating credit budget, asset limits, and per-user rate limit; poll with check_status. Errors clearly on budget exceeded, rate limit, or job creation failure.
Notes: Async job; cost varies by model.

User Story: As an agent, I want to start an async AI image generation job so that I can produce images for posts and carousels.
Expected Behavior: Returns job_id immediately after credit/asset budget and rate-limit checks. Errors on budget exceeded or job creation failure.

User Story: As an agent, I want to poll an async job so that I know when generation completes and get the result URL.
Expected Behavior: Validates job_id format (1-160 alnum), looks up job, fetches live status if processing, returns status/progress/result_url or error_message; masks R2 key in text output.

User Story: As an agent, I want to plan a multi-scene video storyboard so that I can produce cohesive multi-shot content.
Expected Behavior: Builds storyboard prompt with brand context, checks credit budget, calls AI to produce structured JSON frames (shot types, prompts, captions, voiceover).

User Story: As an agent, I want to generate narration audio so that I can add voiceover to videos.
Expected Behavior: Checks credit budget and rate limit, calls TTS edge function, returns audioUrl and durationSeconds; errors on missing URL or budget exceeded.

User Story: As an agent, I want to generate carousel slide text so that I can publish multi-slide Instagram posts.
Expected Behavior: Calculates cost (10 + slide_count*2), checks budget and rate limit, returns slide array (headline, body, emphasis); defaults to hormozi-authority template.

User Story: As a marketer, I want to extract brand identity from a website so that I can generate on-brand content.
Expected Behavior: Validates URL against SSRF blocklist, calls brand-extract EF (60s timeout), returns BrandProfile (name, colors, voice, audience, logo); errors on SSRF or extraction failure.

User Story: As an agent, I want to load the active brand profile so that I can reuse stored brand data.
Expected Behavior: Uses project_id or active context, returns profile record; errors if no project_id and no default configured.

User Story: As a marketer, I want to save/replace the brand profile so that content stays consistent.
Expected Behavior: Defaults project_id to active, validates payload, saves with audit fields, returns saved profile with version; overwrites prior active profile (no merge).

User Story: As a marketer, I want to set platform-specific voice overrides so that each channel sounds right.
Expected Behavior: Updates platform voice (samples, tone, CTA, hashtags) in brand profile; errors if no project_id.

User Story: As an agent, I want the 4-layer brand runtime so that I have messaging, voice, visual, and audience data in one call.
Expected Behavior: Returns messaging/voice/visual/audience layers plus confidence scores as JSON.

User Story: As a marketer, I want to see what brand data exists vs missing so that I know how to improve it.
Expected Behavior: Analyzes 7 sections with completeness scores, reports confidence, gives recommendations.

User Story: As a creator, I want to check content against brand voice so that I catch off-brand copy before publishing.
Expected Behavior: Scores content across voice/vocabulary/messaging/claims (0-100) with issues; errors if no brand profile.

User Story: As a designer, I want to audit colors against the brand palette so that visuals stay compliant.
Expected Behavior: Uses Delta E 2000 distance (default threshold 10), returns per-color compliance and nearest brand color; errors if no palette.

User Story: As a developer, I want to export brand tokens so that I can use them in CSS/Tailwind/Figma.
Expected Behavior: Returns tokens in requested format (css/tailwind/figma); errors if no brand palette.

User Story: As a creator, I want end-to-end carousel creation so that slide text and images are produced together.
Expected Behavior: Injects brand visuals, checks credit/asset budgets, generates text, kicks off parallel image jobs, returns slides + job_ids; partial success returns failedSlides.
Errors Found: brand_id advertised in description + read by handler but MISSING from Zod schema; MCP SDK strips it at runtime so brand auto-injection path was unreachable (also TS2339).
Fix Status: Fixed: added brand_id to input schema (carousel.ts).
Retest Result: Pass (vitest 12/12, verify:lock OK, type error resolved)

User Story: As a creator, I want to score post quality so that I publish only strong content.
Expected Behavior: Scores 7 categories (0-5, max 35) vs threshold (default 26), returns passed flag, per-category scores, blockers.

User Story: As a planner, I want to batch-check a content plan so that I catch weak posts before scheduling.
Expected Behavior: Scores each post, returns per-post details plus aggregate summary (passed/failed/avg).

User Story: As a creator, I want to extract content from a URL so that I can repurpose articles or YouTube transcripts.
Expected Behavior: Validates SSRF, routes YouTube vs article/product, returns ExtractedContent (title, transcript/content, metadata, hooks); errors on SSRF or failure.

User Story: As a marketer, I want a full week content plan so that I have platform-specific drafts and schedule times.
Expected Behavior: Returns ContentPlan with posts, estimated_credits, insights_applied; persists if project_id; source/brand load are non-fatal; AI parse failure returns partial raw.

User Story: As a planner, I want to save a content plan so that I can reference it for approval and scheduling.
Expected Behavior: Returns plan_id/project_id/status; auto-generates plan_id; errors if no project_id and no default.

User Story: As a planner, I want to load a persisted plan so that I can inspect it before editing or scheduling.
Expected Behavior: Returns full plan with posts and approval state; 404 if not found.

User Story: As an editor, I want to edit individual posts in a plan so that I can revise before scheduling.
Expected Behavior: Updates only specified fields per post_id; returns updated count; errors if plan or post_id not found; does not publish.

User Story: As a planner, I want to submit a plan for approval so that posts enter review.
Expected Behavior: Creates pending approval items per post and sets plan to in_review; errors if plan not found or no posts.

User Story: As a creator, I want to publish/schedule a post so that it goes to connected platforms.
Expected Behavior: Auto-rehosts media to R2 (SSRF validated), resolves job_ids, validates caption/title, returns per-platform results; errors if platform not connected.

User Story: As a user, I want to see connected platforms so that I know where I can post.
Expected Behavior: Returns accounts (platform, username, expiry); empty array if none; errors on EF failure.

User Story: As a user, I want to list recent posts so that I can review status and find post IDs.
Expected Behavior: Returns posts filtered by platform/status/days/limit with status icons; empty message if none.

User Story: As a planner, I want optimal non-conflicting posting slots so that I schedule for max engagement.
Expected Behavior: Returns slots sorted by engagement score, avoiding conflicts within min_gap_hours over a 7-day window.

User Story: As a planner, I want to schedule an entire plan so that all approved posts publish on time.
Expected Behavior: Schedules approved/edited posts with auto-slotting, quality gate, batch concurrency, idempotency; supports dry_run; errors if no plan/plan_id.

User Story: As an operator, I want a pre-flight check so that I know the pipeline will succeed before running it.
Expected Behavior: Returns ready flag with checks (credits, accounts, brand, approvals, insights), blockers, warnings.

User Story: As an operator, I want to run the full pipeline so that research-to-scheduling happens in one call.
Expected Behavior: Chains budget->planning->quality->persist->approvals->schedule; supports dry_run/skip_stages; returns pipeline_id and stage results.

User Story: As an operator, I want pipeline run status so that I can track progress and approvals.
Expected Behavior: Returns pipeline object with stages, post counts, credits, errors; message if none found.

User Story: As an operator, I want to auto-approve quality posts so that only weak ones need manual review.
Expected Behavior: Partitions posts into approved/flagged/rejected by threshold, creates approval records, sets plan status.

User Story: As a reviewer, I want approval rows per post so that I have a review queue.
Expected Behavior: Creates one pending row per post; idempotent on (plan_id, post_id); errors if no project_id or empty posts.

User Story: As a reviewer, I want to list approval items so that I know what needs review.
Expected Behavior: Returns items optionally filtered by status with original/edited post data; message if none.

User Story: As a reviewer, I want to approve/reject/edit an item so that I control what publishes.
Expected Behavior: Records decision (edited requires edited_post), immutable once decided; errors on missing edited_post or re-decide.

User Story: As a user, I want a deep link to connect a platform so that I can complete OAuth in the browser.
Expected Behavior: Returns nonce/platform/expires_at/deep_link (2-min expiry); errors on EF failure or missing link.

User Story: As a user, I want to poll until a connection is active so that I know when I can post.
Expected Behavior: Polls until account active or timeout (default 30s, max 60s); returns connected flag/account details or timed_out.

User Story: As a creator, I want to generate platform-tailored content so that I have a ready draft.
Expected Behavior: Loads brand/ideation context (non-fatal), applies platform voice overrides, returns generated text; rate-limited.

User Story: As a creator, I want trending topics so that I have content inspiration.
Expected Behavior: Returns trends from youtube/google_trends/rss/url with 1-hour cache; SSRF validated; errors if url missing for rss/url source.

User Story: As a creator, I want to rewrite content for another platform so that one message works everywhere.
Expected Behavior: Applies per-platform guidelines (length, hashtags, CTA, tone) and voice overrides; rate-limited; non-fatal brand load.

User Story: As an agent, I want synthesized ideation context so that generation reflects past performance.
Expected Behavior: Returns IdeationContext (promptInjection, recommended model/duration/time, winning patterns, top hooks); empty defaults if no data; errors if no project.

User Story: As a creator, I want data-driven content suggestions so that I know what to make next.
Expected Behavior: Returns suggestions with confidence and rationale from insights/swipes/format gaps; no AI/credit cost; pads to count.

User Story: As a creator, I want to upload media to durable storage so that I get a stable r2_key for posting.
Expected Behavior: Validates file types, 10MB base64 cap, sensitive paths, SSRF; returns r2_key/signed_url/size; presigned PUT for large files.

User Story: As a creator, I want a fresh signed URL for an R2 key so that I can access stored media.
Expected Behavior: Returns signed_url with 1-hour expiry; errors from EF or no URL returned.

User Story: As a creator, I want to list video compositions so that I can pick a template.
Expected Behavior: Returns static list of compositions with resolution/fps/duration/use case. No errors.

User Story: As a developer, I want to render a composition locally so that I can preview output.
Expected Behavior: Renders to public/videos/ (mp4/gif), returns path/resolution; errors on unknown id, bad JSON props, render failure.

User Story: As a creator, I want to render a template video in the cloud so that I get a hosted MP4.
Expected Behavior: Creates async job, returns job_id/content_history_id/credits_charged; errors on unknown id or bad props.

User Story: As a developer, I want to screenshot an app page so that I can verify UI states.
Expected Behavior: Logs in with test creds, captures full-page PNG to screenshots dir; errors on unknown page, SSRF, browser failure.

User Story: As a developer, I want to screenshot any public URL so that I can capture external pages.
Expected Behavior: SSRF + DNS-pin protection, returns PNG path; errors on SSRF, invalid output_path, browser failure.

User Story: As a marketer, I want post performance metrics so that I can measure results.
Expected Behavior: Returns totals and per-post breakdown filtered by platform/days/content_id; message if no data.

User Story: As a marketer, I want to queue an analytics refresh so that metrics update from platforms.
Expected Behavior: Queues refresh for last 7 days, returns processed/queued/errored counts; rate-limited.

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want channel/video analytics so that I can track growth.
Expected Behavior: Returns metrics per action (channel/daily/video/topVideos); errors if video_id missing for video action.

User Story: As a marketer, I want aggregated insights so that I can learn what works.
Expected Behavior: Returns insights array (type, data, confidence, generated_at) with optional type filter.

User Story: As a marketer, I want best posting times so that I schedule for engagement.
Expected Behavior: Returns top 5 slots (day/hour, avg engagement, sample size); message if no data.

User Story: As a marketer, I want a performance summary so that I get trends and recommendations.
Expected Behavior: Returns metrics, trends vs previous period, top/bottom performers, recommendations; no AI/credit cost.

User Story: As a marketer, I want anomaly detection so that I notice spikes/drops/viral content.
Expected Behavior: Compares current vs previous period at chosen sensitivity, returns anomalies with suggested actions; message if none.

User Story: As an operator, I want to list autopilot configs so that I can review schedules and budgets.
Expected Behavior: Returns configs with schedule/budgets/last-run/status; message if none.

User Story: As an operator, I want to create an autopilot config so that the pipeline runs automatically.
Expected Behavior: Creates config (schedule, budgets, approval mode), returns config; rate-limited.

User Story: As an operator, I want to update an autopilot config so that I can change schedule/budget/active state.
Expected Behavior: Updates given fields, returns config; errors if no changes specified; rate-limited.

User Story: As an operator, I want autopilot status so that I see active configs and recent runs.
Expected Behavior: Returns activeConfigs, pendingApprovals, recentRuns; handles empty state.

User Story: As a user, I want to list recipe templates so that I can pick a workflow.
Expected Behavior: Returns recipes with metadata/inputs/steps filtered by category/featured; message if none.

User Story: As a user, I want full recipe details so that I know steps and required inputs.
Expected Behavior: Returns full recipe object; errors if not found (case-sensitive slug).

User Story: As a user, I want to execute a recipe so that a multi-step workflow runs.
Expected Behavior: Creates run, returns run_id/status (async); errors on invalid slug or EF failure.

User Story: As a user, I want recipe run status so that I can track progress and outputs.
Expected Behavior: Returns run with status/progress/current_step/credits/outputs; errors if run not found.

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want to list comments so that I can engage with my audience.
Expected Behavior: Returns comments (with pagination) for a video or recent across channel; message if none.

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want to post a comment so that I can engage on my videos.
Expected Behavior: Posts top-level comment, returns id/textDisplay; rate-limited (anti-spam).

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want to reply to a comment so that I can respond to viewers.
Expected Behavior: Posts reply to parent_id, returns id/textDisplay; rate-limited.

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want to moderate comments so that I control what is public.
Expected Behavior: Sets status published/rejected, returns success; rate-limited.

User Story: As a YouTuber, I want to delete a comment so that I can remove my own comments.
Expected Behavior: Deletes owned comment, returns success; rate-limited.

User Story: As a user, I want to check credit balance so that I can budget operations.
Expected Behavior: Returns balance, monthlyUsed, monthlyLimit, plan; errors on EF failure.

User Story: As an agent, I want per-session budget consumption so that I avoid overspending in a run.
Expected Behavior: Returns credits/assets used vs caps this run from in-memory state. No errors.

User Story: As a user, I want monthly MCP usage so that I see per-tool call counts and credits.
Expected Behavior: Returns totalCalls/totalCredits and per-tool breakdown; message if none.

User Story: As an agent, I want to search available tools so that I can discover capabilities efficiently.
Expected Behavior: Returns filtered tools by query/module/scope at chosen detail level; always valid (empty if no match).

User Story: As a user, I want a one-call dashboard so that I see brand/content/insights and next action.
Expected Behavior: Returns brandStatus, recentContent, currentInsights, recommendedNextAction; errors if no project.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to publish a post so that I can distribute content from the terminal.
Expected Behavior: Requires --media-url/--caption/--platforms/--confirm; returns idempotency key, scheduled time, per-platform results; exit 1 on error.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to score content so that I gate quality before publishing.
Expected Behavior: Returns score/categories/blockers; exit 1 if below threshold, 0 if passes.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want end-to-end validate+publish so that preflight, quality, and posting run together.
Expected Behavior: Runs preflight + quality then publishes if passing; --dry-run/--force bypass; exit 1 if blockers and not forced.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to list/view/approve plans so that I manage content plans.
Expected Behavior: list/view/approve subcommands; missing plan-id errors; unknown subcommand shows usage.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to manage presets so that I reuse platform format limits.
Expected Behavior: list/show/save/delete; saves to user config; cannot overwrite/delete builtins.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to check OAuth token health so that I avoid expired connections.
Expected Behavior: Returns per-platform status/expiry/refresh presence; exit 1 if any inactive/expired/missing refresh.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to refresh OAuth tokens so that connections stay valid.
Expected Behavior: Refreshes given/all platforms, returns new expiry; exit 1 if any fails.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to validate account readiness so that I know if I can publish.
Expected Behavior: Checks privacy/terms URLs, credit caps, OAuth; exit 1 if blocking check fails.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to list recent posts so that I review status from the terminal.
Expected Behavior: Returns posts filtered by days/platform/status; empty message if none.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to queue analytics refresh so that metrics update.
Expected Behavior: Returns count of posts queued; throws on EF failure.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want feedback loop status so that I see brand/content/insights and next action.
Expected Behavior: Returns hasProfile, recentContent, currentInsights, recommendedNextAction; degrades gracefully.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to poll job status so that I track async jobs.
Expected Behavior: Returns job details; exit 1 if job not found.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want autopilot status so that I see configs and approvals.
Expected Behavior: Returns activeConfigs/pendingApprovals/configs; 0 if none.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want monthly usage so that I track tool calls and credits.
Expected Behavior: Returns totals and per-tool breakdown; falls back to activity_logs if RPC unavailable.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want credit balance so that I budget from the terminal.
Expected Behavior: Returns balance/monthlyUsed/monthlyLimit/plan; 0 balance if query fails.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want to discover tools so that I see available MCP capabilities.
Expected Behavior: Returns tools filtered by scope/module grouped by module; message if none match.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want server info so that I see version/auth/credits.
Expected Behavior: Returns version, toolCount, modules, auth, creditBalance; omits auth/credits gracefully if offline.

User Story: As a client, I want to init a session or send JSON-RPC so that I can use MCP over HTTP.
Expected Behavior: Returns Mcp-Session-Id and JSON-RPC result; 4h TTL/30m idle; 400/403/429/440/401 on respective errors.

User Story: As a client, I want an SSE stream so that I can long-poll an existing session.
Expected Behavior: Returns SSE stream with no-buffering header; 400/403/440 on errors.

User Story: As a client, I want to close a session so that I free resources.
Expected Behavior: Returns 200 session_closed; 400/403 on missing/foreign session.

User Story: As an operator, I want a liveness probe so that load balancers can health-check.
Expected Behavior: Returns 200 {status:ok, version}. No auth.

User Story: As an operator, I want detailed health so that I see sessions/uptime/memory.
Expected Behavior: Returns detailed stats; 401 if token invalid.

User Story: As a directory, I want server discovery metadata so that I can list the server.
Expected Behavior: Returns static server card (info, auth, tools/prompts/resources); cached 1h. No auth.

User Story: As a stdio client, I want public connection config so that I can connect in cloud mode.
Expected Behavior: Returns {supabaseUrl, anonKey}; no secrets; cached 1h. No auth.

User Story: As a client, I want OAuth 2.1 endpoints so that I can authenticate.
Expected Behavior: Returns auth codes/tokens/registration per spec; OAuth error responses on failure.

User Story: As a client, I want AS metadata so that I can auto-discover OAuth.
Expected Behavior: Returns protected-resource metadata; 404 if unavailable. No auth.

User Story: As an agent, I want the brand profile resource so that I can read brand voice data.
Expected Behavior: Returns JSON profile; no_profile status with guidance if missing; error/denied statuses handled. Scope mcp:read.

User Story: As an agent, I want the account overview resource so that I can read plan/credits/connections.
Expected Behavior: Returns plan/credits/status/links; error/denied statuses handled. Scope mcp:read.

User Story: As an agent, I want capabilities reference so that I know platforms/models/costs.
Expected Behavior: Returns static JSON (platforms, formats, models, costs, tiers). Public.

User Story: As an agent, I want a quick start guide so that I learn common workflows.
Expected Behavior: Returns markdown guide. Public.

User Story: As a user, I want a weekly content plan prompt so that the agent orchestrates planning tools.
Expected Behavior: Returns user message guiding generate_content/get_brand_profile/insights/best-times/save_content_plan. Params: niche (req), platforms, tone.

User Story: As a user, I want a top-content analysis prompt so that the agent finds winning patterns.
Expected Behavior: Returns message guiding fetch_analytics/insights/best-times for top posts and recommendations. Params: timeframe, platform.

User Story: As a user, I want a repurposing prompt so that one source becomes many posts.
Expected Behavior: Returns message guiding generate_content/get_brand_profile/quality_check across platforms. Params: source (req), target_platforms.

User Story: As a user, I want a brand-voice setup prompt so that the agent defines and saves a profile.
Expected Behavior: Returns message guiding profile definition then save_brand_profile/generate_content. Params: brand_name (req), industry, website.

User Story: As a user, I want a content audit prompt so that the agent produces a prioritized action plan.
Expected Behavior: Returns message guiding credit/analytics/insights/brand/best-times into an audit report. No params.

User Story: As a user, I want a visual weekly calendar so that I can view, create, and reschedule posts.
Expected Behavior: Loads week posts + scopes, week grid, platform filters, drilldown, drag-drop reschedule (write scope), suggest-slot, quick-create modal with validations, accessible toasts.
Notes: Read-only shows upgrade banner without mcp:distribute.
